<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> because he knows something that we need to remember.<|5.42|>
<|6.86|> That paradigm, that way of being is a lie.<|11.80|>
<|13.50|> Jesus rejects that crime.<|17.00|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Instead, we see him portray for us the true model of kingship, the one we know in our<|10.68|>
<|10.68|> hearts that fulfills that age-old motif when he shows himself to be the Good Shepherd whom<|20.18|>
<|20.18|> Jeremiah prophesied about.<|22.20|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.80|> This good shepherd, this king, reconciles everything to himself and sets it all free.<|10.20|>
<|10.94|> He shows us how, as his disciples, we are to do likewise.<|15.92|>
<|16.58|> In his life, we see him walk with his friends and love them till the end and even beyond it.<|26.80|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> But it's not just his friends. He walks even with those who called themselves his enemies.<|8.64|>
<|9.66|> And he loves them too until the end and yes, even beyond it.<|16.14|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Just before his crucifixion, Pilate asks him, "Are you a king?"<|8.24|>
<|8.24|> Jesus gives him what must have been the most infuriating answer, "You say that I am."<|16.40|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Infuriating or not, the truth is in that answer and we need it.<|6.30|>
<|6.30|> Because Pilate didn't know what a real king was.<|10.68|>
<|10.68|> He'd never seen one.<|13.18|>
<|13.18|> Or at least he thought he hadn't.<|15.94|>
<|15.94|> But there, right in front of him, was the real king.<|20.30|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And when he ascended to that cross and was enthroned,<|5.00|>
<|5.00|> he looked out at the entire world and said,<|9.84|>
<|9.84|> "Forgive them, they know not what they do."<|13.52|>
<|13.52|> That's what we remember today when we celebrate Christ the King,<|19.52|>
<|19.52|> that here is the righteous branch,<|21.82|>
<|21.82|> the one who reigns as King and deals wisely,<|24.82|>
<|24.82|> who executes justice and righteousness.<|27.70|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> This is the sort of life that we are to live.<|4.16|>
<|4.66|> This is the way that we are to walk.<|7.22|>
<|7.98|> Because it is the way, the way that leads to an everlasting peace, even now.<|19.14|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Amen.<|0.10|><|endoftext|>
